skill.name: semantic-consistency-auditor
skill.description: Evaluates semantic consistency between AI-generated clinical notes and expert gold-standard text using BERTScore and COMET algorithms.
skill.summary: NLP-based semantic evaluation tool for academic writing that measures text similarity using deep learning models.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 79 potential issues, but all are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code span formatting, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference BERTScore and COMET NLP algorithms in documentation and help text, not cryptographic algorithms. The 'system reconnaissance' findings are standard error messages and documentation. The 'hidden file' finding documents a configuration file path. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns were found. This skill is a legitimate academic writing evaluation tool with no security threats.

low_findings.title: Documentation mentions hidden home directory config path
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents a configuration file path at ~/.openclaw/skills/semantic-consistency-auditor/config.yaml. This is documentation only, not actual file access by code. The main.py script uses a config_path parameter that defaults to None and falls back to DEFAULT_CONFIG, so no automatic home directory access occurs.

low_findings.title: File write to user-specified output path
low_findings.description: scripts/main.py line 635 opens a file for writing at the user-specified --output path. This is standard behavior for a CLI tool that saves results to a file. No suspicious paths or hidden locations are used.

content.user_title: Assess Semantic Consistency of AI-Generated Clinical Notes
content.value_statement: Academic writers and medical researchers need to verify that AI-generated text accurately preserves the meaning of expert-written reference text. This skill uses BERTScore and COMET deep learning algorithms to measure semantic consistency between candidate text and gold-standard references, providing objective similarity scores.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates semantic similarity between AI-generated text and reference text using BERTScore precision, recall, and F1 metrics
Assesses semantic entailment with COMET neural network model for deep meaning comparison
Processes single text pairs via command-line arguments or batch JSON files for bulk evaluation
Generates detailed reports with grade classifications and pass/fail decisions based on configurable thresholds
Supports multiple languages including Chinese and English for cross-language text evaluation
Computes summary statistics and grade distributions for batch evaluation results
limitations: Requires 1-2 GB of disk space for downloading BERTScore and COMET models on first run
GPU is strongly recommended for acceptable performance on batch evaluations of large datasets
Evaluates semantic consistency only and cannot verify factual accuracy or clinical correctness
Model download and loading can take several minutes without a cached model

use_cases.title: Validate Clinical Note Quality
use_cases.description: A medical researcher processes AI-generated clinical notes against expert gold-standard records to verify that key medical information is semantically preserved.

use_cases.title: Audit Academic Writing Consistency
use_cases.description: An academic editor checks whether rewritten or summarized passages maintain semantic equivalence with the original source text before publication.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Medical Translation Output
use_cases.description: A language specialist uses semantic scoring to compare translated medical content against source documents to ensure meaning is preserved across languages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic single-pair evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Evaluate the semantic consistency between the following AI-generated text and the gold-standard reference using BERTScore and COMET.

AI-generated text: [insert text]
Gold-standard reference: [insert text]

Run the evaluation and report the BERTScore F1, COMET score, and the overall pass/fail result.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user evaluating a single pair of texts

prompt_templates.title: Batch file evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a JSON file at [filepath] containing multiple case records with ai_generated and gold_standard fields. Run batch evaluation using the semantic consistency auditor, output the results in detailed format to [output path], and provide a summary of the pass rate and grade distribution.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with a pre-prepared batch JSON file of cases

prompt_templates.title: Custom threshold configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the semantic consistency of these texts using stricter criteria. Set the BERTScore F1 threshold to 0.90, COMET score threshold to 0.80, and overall consistency threshold to 0.85. Use the detailed output format and save results to [output path].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requiring stricter quality criteria

prompt_templates.title: Python API integration for custom workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SemanticConsistencyAuditor Python class to integrate semantic evaluation into a custom workflow. Initialize with bert_model="microsoft/deberta-xlarge-mnli" and lang="en", then call evaluate_batch() on the loaded cases. Extract the metrics and generate a formatted report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer embedding evaluation into a larger pipeline

output_examples.input: Single clinical note comparison: AI-generated text describing patient fever symptoms compared against expert gold-standard text with similar but differently worded content.
output_examples.output: The evaluation returned a BERTScore F1 of 0.9028 and a COMET score of 0.8234, resulting in a semantic consistency score of 0.8631. The system classified the result as "good" and marked it as passed. The semantic analysis identified matched concepts including fever duration and temperature, with no critical gaps detected.

output_examples.input: Batch evaluation of 100 clinical case pairs from a JSON file, comparing AI-generated admission notes against expert gold standards.
output_examples.output: The batch evaluation processed all 100 cases and returned an 85% pass rate. The average BERTScore F1 was 0.8923 and the average COMET score was 0.8234 across all cases. The grade distribution showed 30 excellent, 40 good, 15 pass, and 15 needing improvement. The system identified the specific cases that failed threshold checks for targeted review.

best_practices: Always verify that reference gold-standard text is accurate and approved by domain experts before running evaluation
Use batch mode for multiple cases to maximize GPU utilization and reduce per-case processing overhead
Adjust consistency thresholds based on domain requirements, using stricter thresholds for clinical documentation and looser thresholds for exploratory text
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to evaluate factual accuracy or clinical correctness of medical claims, as it only measures semantic similarity
Do not run evaluation on empty, placeholder, or template text as this produces meaningless similarity scores
Do not rely on a single algorithm score for critical decisions without reviewing the detailed semantic gap analysis

faq.question: What is the difference between BERTScore and COMET?
faq.answer: BERTScore measures token-level similarity using contextual embeddings from BERT models, providing precision, recall, and F1 scores. COMET is a neural network trained on human judgments that produces a single semantic quality score. Using both together gives a more complete evaluation.

faq.question: Can I evaluate non-medical text with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The algorithms work on any text pair in supported languages. The skill was designed for clinical notes but the underlying BERTScore and COMET models work on general text.

faq.question: How long does the first evaluation take?
faq.answer: The first run downloads models weighing 1-2 GB total. This can take several minutes depending on internet speed. Subsequent evaluations run much faster with cached models.

faq.question: What do the grade classifications mean?
faq.answer: Grades map to semantic consistency scores: excellent (0.90+), good (0.80-0.89), pass (0.70-0.79), needs improvement (0.60-0.69), and unqualified (below 0.60). Thresholds are configurable.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a GPU?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool runs on CPU by default and falls back automatically if CUDA is not available. CPU evaluation is slower, especially for large batches, but produces the same results.

faq.question: What file format does batch input require?
faq.answer: Batch input uses JSON format with an array of objects. Each object should contain ai_generated and gold_standard string fields and an optional case_id field. See the SKILL.md documentation for the exact format.
